entities.name: OAuth 2.0 Authorization Code Flow
entities.context: The code implements the standard OAuth 2.0 authorization code grant type for token exchange

entities.name: HTTP POST
entities.context: Used via http_post helper to make token exchange requests

entities.name: Base64 Basic Auth
entities.context: Client ID and secret are Base64-encoded for the Authorization header

entities.name: TOKEN_URL
entities.context: Constant representing the OAuth token endpoint URL

entities.name: exchange_code_for_tokens
entities.context: Function that exchanges an authorization code for access and refresh tokens

entities.name: refresh_tokens
entities.context: Function that refreshes expired access tokens using a refresh token

entities.name: save_config
entities.context: Persists token credentials (access_token, refresh_token, expiry) to configuration storage

entities.name: JSON
entities.context: Used to parse HTTP responses from the token endpoint

entities.name: HTTPError
entities.context: Exception handling for failed HTTP requests with readable error bodies

relationships.context: Implements the token exchange step of the OAuth 2.0 authorization code grant
Encodes client_id:client_secret as Basic auth header for token request
Sends form-encoded POST to TOKEN_URL to obtain tokens
Persists obtained tokens after successful exchange
Handles the token refresh phase of the OAuth lifecycle
Uses same Basic auth pattern for refresh requests
Responses from token endpoint are parsed as JSON
HTTP errors are caught and surfaced with decoded error bodies
